animeDestinyCodes.title: Anime Destiny Codes
animeDestinyCodes.intro: Use Anime Destiny codes before summoning so your first Gems and reroll resources go into stronger early progress. Active codes are shown first so you can claim rewards before your first banner roll.

activeCodes.note: Best first code to redeem before summoning units.
Strong starter bundle for summons and early reroll resources.

redeemSteps: Open Anime Destiny from the official Roblox experience page.
Click the Codes button on the right side of the screen.
Paste the code exactly as written, including capital letters and punctuation.
Press Redeem and check your Gems, RR, Cubes, or Malice balance.

rewardTypes.name: Gems
rewardTypes.use: Main currency for summoning units across banners.

rewardTypes.name: RR
rewardTypes.use: Trait reroll resource used to change unit traits.

rewardTypes.name: Cubes
rewardTypes.use: Upgrade resource spent through in-game upgrade systems.

rewardTypes.name: Malice
rewardTypes.use: Launch reward resource kept for the system that requires it.

animeDestinyBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Destiny Beginner Guide
animeDestinyBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime Destiny is a Roblox anime tower defense game where you summon anime units, upgrade and evolve them, clear story stages, challenge bosses, and test squads in endless trials. Focus your first day on collecting free rewards, summoning a usable team, learning upgrade timing, and saving trait rerolls for units that stay useful later.

steps.title: Launch the Official Game
steps.description: Start from the official Roblox page and join the Anime Destiny group for update and reward access. The game supports mobile, tablet, PC, and console, and Auto Roll is free.
steps.tip: Open Anime Destiny on Roblox, join the group, and check the in-game lobby before spending currency.

steps.title: Redeem Codes First
steps.description: Redeem Release! and SorryForDelay before your first serious summon session. These codes give Gems for summons and RR for trait rerolls.
steps.tip: Use the Codes button on the right side of the screen, then claim rewards before rolling.

steps.title: Summon a Starter Squad
steps.description: Use Gems to summon units and build a balanced early squad instead of chasing only one rare pull. Story progress matters more than a perfect first banner roll.
steps.tip: Equip your strongest available units and keep enough Gems for future banners.

steps.title: Upgrade During Battles
steps.description: Place units where they cover enemy paths, then upgrade your best damage units first. Bosses and harder waves punish weak placement.
steps.tip: Focus upgrades on units that clear waves, break shields, or help stop bosses.

steps.title: Clear Story Stages
steps.description: Story stages unlock progression, harder content, and farming routes. Finish story before judging the full strength of late-game units.
steps.tip: Push story stages steadily and return to earlier content when you need resources.

steps.title: Save Trait Rerolls
steps.description: Do not spend most RR on early Rare, Epic, or temporary Legendary units. Rerolls have better long-term value on Mythic and Secret units.
steps.tip: Use only light rerolling early, then save the bulk of RR for stronger units.

steps.title: Farm Repeatable Rewards
steps.description: Daily, weekly, 15-minute, and 30-minute challenges, Index rewards, Milestone rewards, Namak Planet Hard, and the Igris Boss Event are important resource routes.
steps.tip: Use repeatable content to collect Gems, BattlePass XP, Stat Cubes, and RR.

animeDestinyUnitTierList.title: Anime Destiny Unit Tier List
animeDestinyUnitTierList.intro: This Anime Destiny tier list focuses on evolved units at high investment, with extra weight given to DPS, shield breaking, boss damage, summon value, and utility. Early units help during story, but Mythic and Secret units are the main long-term investment targets.

units.name: Frozen Moon (Kizuki)
units.note: Strong mix of damage and freeze-based stall utility with excellent shield-breaking value.

units.name: Founding Titan (Queen)
units.role: Full AOE damage, summon damage, boss killer
units.note: High boss damage and strong utility from Titan summons.

units.name: Shadow Monarch (Awaken)
units.role: Base damage, summon damage, dark synergy
units.note: Strong summon-based unit with damage synergy alongside Crimson Knight (Dark).

units.name: Crimson Knight (Dark)
units.note: A strong Secret unit that performs especially well with Shadow Monarch synergy.

units.name: Ashura Swordsman (3SS)
units.note: One of the strongest Mythic DPS units for damage-over-time and shield pressure.

units.name: Red Emperor (Haki)
units.note: One of the best Mythic units for high-end damage investment.

units.name: Eternal Night (Blood)
units.note: Strong boss-killer option with damage that benefits from a crit-focused setup.

units.name: Future Warrior (Furious)
units.note: Reliable single-target damage unit for boss-focused fights.

units.name: Insect Slayer (Poison)
units.note: Good damage-over-time alternative when top S-tier DoT options are unavailable.

units.name: Magicbane (Anti-Magic)
units.note: Useful when stages demand reliable shield-breaking pressure.

units.name: Radiant Hunter (Light)
units.note: Solid single-target damage but lower overall output than higher-tier Mythics and Secrets.

units.name: Destruction God (Mogger)
units.note: Decent damage, but its cost-to-output value is weaker than top-tier units.

units.name: Yellow Flash
units.note: Good for early progress, then replace when stronger Mythic or Secret units arrive.

units.name: Hawkeye
units.note: Useful for early stages but not a priority for heavy RR investment.

units.name: Corrupted Warrior
units.note: A starter-friendly unit that falls off later.

units.name: Crow Prodigy
units.note: Useful early shield-breaking option before Mythic units replace it.

units.name: Most Rare and Epic Units
units.note: Use them only while building your first squad, then replace them as soon as stronger units are available.

animeDestinyTraitsAndReroll.title: Anime Destiny Traits and Reroll Guide
animeDestinyTraitsAndReroll.intro: Traits improve unit performance and RR is the key reroll resource in Anime Destiny. The best early rule is simple: use small reroll amounts on temporary units, save serious RR spending for Mythic and Secret units, and match traits to the unit role instead of blindly chasing one label.

rows.advice: Save most RR for units that remain useful in boss fights, story clears, and late-game content.

rows.bestFor: High-end DPS units and top Secret or Mythic carries
rows.advice: Keep this on main damage carries and avoid replacing it casually.
Keep it as a strong DPS option when the unit benefits from damage-over-time pressure.
Use it on units built for boss damage or crit-based single-target output.
Keep it on economy or support units instead of forcing pure DPS traits onto them.
Accept it as an early useful roll, but do not spend heavy RR chasing perfect traits on temporary units.
Collect from code rewards and farming routes, then spend through the relevant in-game upgrade systems.
Claim from the SorryForDelay code and keep it for the in-game system that requires it.

animeDestinySummonAndGems.title: Anime Destiny Summon and Gems Guide
animeDestinySummonAndGems.intro: Anime Destiny is built around summoning anime units, forging an ultimate squad, and improving that squad through upgrades and evolutions. Treat summons as roster planning, so every Gem goes toward a clear squad goal instead of a random roll.

cards.name: Summon Units
cards.description: Summon powerful anime units to expand your roster and create a squad that can handle Story stages, bosses, and Endless Trials.
cards.action: Fill a usable squad first, then chase stronger or rarer units after your basic team can clear early content.

cards.name: Gems Budgeting
cards.description: Use Gems as your summon budget and decide what your squad actually needs before spending.
cards.action: Save for meaningful summon sessions instead of spending every small amount immediately.

cards.name: Free Auto Roll
cards.description: The Roblox game page lists Auto Roll as free, making repeated rolling easier during long summon sessions.
cards.action: Use Auto Roll when you are ready to commit resources to a banner and review the results afterward.

cards.name: Squad Building After Summons
cards.description: Every summon can change your roster plan because new units may improve damage, coverage, or consistency.
cards.action: Upgrade the units you actively use before spreading resources across every new pull.

tips: Redeem Anime Destiny codes for free Gems before your first summon session.
Keep a Gem reserve so new Mythic or Secret banners do not catch you empty-handed.
Use Auto Roll while farming, but check results before investing upgrade resources.
Prioritize banners that fill a missing role like boss damage, wave clear, or shield breaking.

animeDestinyEvolutionMaterials.title: Anime Destiny Evolution and Upgrade Materials
animeDestinyEvolutionMaterials.intro: Track the materials each Anime Destiny unit needs and farm them in a sensible order, so upgrades, evolutions, and ability unlocks go into the units that stay useful through Story stages, bosses, and Endless Trials.

rows.use: Increasing the power of units already placed in your active squad.
rows.source: In-game unit upgrade panel and Anime Destiny Trello resource sections.
rows.bestUse: Focus on units that help clear Story stages and boss fights.

rows.use: Evolving eligible units and opening stronger ability paths for core units.
rows.source: Each unit evolution recipe in-game and organized Trello unit entries.
rows.bestUse: Farm materials for units you plan to keep instead of every new summon.

rows.use: Unlocking stronger attacks or effects after upgrade and evolution progress.
rows.source: Unit detail panels and Trello system notes.
rows.bestUse: Prioritize abilities that improve wave clear, boss damage, or team consistency.

rows.use: Extra resources tied to updates, group activity, and reward announcements.
rows.source: Discord announcements, Roblox group posts, and Trello update notes.
rows.bestUse: Claim timed rewards before spending farmed materials.

animeDestinyStoryBossesEndless.title: Anime Destiny Story Stages, Bosses, and Endless Trials
animeDestinyStoryBossesEndless.intro: Anime Destiny PvE progression runs through Story stages, boss fights, and Endless Trials. Use Story stages to test your early squad, bosses to check focused damage and preparation, and Endless Trials to measure long-run consistency before pushing harder content.

items.title: Story Stages
items.summary: Story stages are the core PvE path for early progression and squad testing in Anime Destiny.
details: Build a reliable squad before rushing every new stage.
Upgrade active units when waves start surviving too long.
Use Story progress to decide whether your roster needs more damage, coverage, or support.

items.title: Boss Challenges
items.summary: Boss fights test whether your squad can handle stronger threats instead of only clearing normal waves.
details: Enter boss content after your main squad has basic upgrades.
Bring units that can keep damage on high-health targets.
Use boss attempts to decide which evolution or upgrade materials deserve priority.

items.title: Endless Trials
items.summary: Endless Trials are repeatable long-run content for checking how far your squad can survive.
details: Move into Endless after Story progression feels stable.
Prioritize sustained damage, coverage, and upgraded core units.
Track where runs fail, then improve the unit type causing the bottleneck.

items.title: Solo and Team Play
items.summary: Anime Destiny supports solo play and teaming up with friends for greater threats.
details: Use solo play to learn placement, pacing, and upgrade needs.
Use team play when harder content needs more coverage or damage.
Coordinate roles so every player covers a different job instead of overlapping.

animeDestinyOfficialLinks.title: Anime Destiny Official Links and Update Tracker
animeDestinyOfficialLinks.intro: Bookmark the official Anime Destiny links to avoid fake mirrors, expired invite reposts, and third-party pages pretending to be guides. Play on Roblox, join the group, follow Discord announcements, and check the Trello board for unit and update reference.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Anime Destiny

questions.question: What is Anime Destiny?
questions.answer: Anime Destiny is a Roblox anime tower defense game where you summon anime units, upgrade and evolve them, and clear story stages, bosses, and endless trials.

questions.question: Where can I play Anime Destiny?
questions.answer: You can play Anime Destiny on the official Roblox experience page. The game supports mobile, tablet, PC, and console.

questions.question: How do I get free rewards in Anime Destiny?
questions.answer: Redeem Anime Destiny codes from the Codes button in-game. Active codes give Gems, RR, Cubes, and Malice to boost your early progress.

questions.question: Is Auto Roll free in Anime Destiny?
questions.answer: Yes. Auto Roll is free in Anime Destiny, so you can run repeated summons automatically while building your squad.

questions.question: What are the best units in Anime Destiny?
questions.answer: Mythic and Secret units are the main long-term investment targets in Anime Destiny. Check the Anime Destiny Unit Tier List for ranked picks across story, bosses, and late game.

questions.question: Where are official Anime Destiny updates posted?
questions.answer: Official Anime Destiny updates are shared in the Discord server and Roblox group. Bookmark the official links to avoid fake mirrors and expired invite reposts.
